metadata.title: Build Quantum Gates on the Bloch Sphere with Claude Code + Manim
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = Bloch vector/state; CRIMSON = gate annotations. INK for all text.
metadata.note: Medhavy register. B00=brand intro; B01-B04=gate Bloch simulation; B05=brand outro.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B05: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Nik Bear Brown — Claude Code, Manim, and the physics, taken apart.

The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — write a Manim scene, Bloch sphere. Show the qubit state zero at the north pole. Apply the Hadamard gate — rotate to the equator, state plus. Then apply the S gate — rotate ninety degrees in the equatorial plane to state i. Annotate each rotation with the gate name. Three things: the rule, the numbers, what to render.

props.command:
claude "write a Manim scene: Bloch sphere
  |0⟩ at north pole → apply H → |+⟩ at equator
  then apply S gate → |i⟩=(|0⟩+i|1⟩)/√2
  annotate each rotation with gate label"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes the file. Before running — read it. Find the H matrix: one over root-two times the matrix with rows one-one and one-minus-one. Multiply it by the zero state column vector. You get plus. That is the line that moves the Bloch vector from pole to equator. Check that H times H equals the identity — if not, the gate is wrong. Thirty seconds.

props.code:
# gates.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np

# Hadamard gate  [← check this matrix]
H = np.array([[1,1],[1,-1]]) / np.sqrt(2)

# Phase (S) gate  [← 90° z-rotation]
S = np.array([[1,0],[0,1j]])

# State |0> = [1, 0]
ket0 = np.array([1, 0], dtype=complex)
ket_plus = H @ ket0    # = [1,1]/√2  ✓
ket_i    = S @ ket_plus # = [1,i]/√2  ✓

# Verify: H @ H == I
print(np.allclose(H @ H, np.eye(2)))  # True

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two things to verify. First: the Hadamard rotation. State zero starts at the north pole of the Bloch sphere. After H, the vector lies on the equator, pointing along the positive x-axis. That is state plus. Second: the S gate. From the equator, the vector rotates ninety degrees in the equatorial plane to point along the positive y-axis. That is state i. Both rotations are exact. No approximation. Both checks pass.

Iterate: claude — update gates dot py — apply Hadamard a second time and show the vector returns to the north pole. H times H equals identity. One gate, one verification, one re-run. The loop closes. The physics was the vehicle. The loop is the skill.

props.command:
claude "update gates.py:
  → apply H a second time after S
  → show vector returns toward |0⟩
  → verify H∘H = I on screen"

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational A-I. On YouTube, at Nik Bear Brown. Nik Bear Brown dot com.
